title: Democratic elitism: the founding myth of American political science [Book Review]
author: Arlen

opening_move.description: The review opens with Piano's thesis about 'mistranslation': American political science misread the Italian elitists (Pareto, Mosca, Michels) by converting their critique of liberal democracy into a justification for it, thereby constructing 'democratic elitism' as a founding myth.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: The ideological construction of 'democratic elitism' has distorted both American political science and democratic theory by naturalising elite rule under the guise of empirical realism

theoretical_positioning.description: The review presents Piano's genealogical argument appreciatively, contextualising the misappropriation of Italian elitism by American social scientists.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: intellectual history; genealogical analysis of political science canon formation
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. Piano's book itself uses historical-archival methods to reconstruct the reception of Italian elitists.

case_type.case_name: Italian elitists (Pareto, Mosca, Michels) and their American reception; Piano, Democratic Elitism (2025)

normative_stake.domain: democratic theory / disciplinary history of political science

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository and appreciative. The genealogical argument belongs to Piano; the reviewer presents and contextualises it.
